purpose.summary: This copybook defines the structure PAUTBPCB, which is likely used to hold PCB (Program Communication Block) information related to IMS database interaction. It contains fields for database name, segment level, PCB status, processing options, segment name, key feedback length, number of sensitive segments, and key feedback area.

paragraphs.purpose: This copybook defines the data structure PAUTBPCB, which is used to store information retrieved from or passed to an IMS Program Communication Block (PCB). The PCB provides a communication interface between a COBOL program and the IMS database. The PAUT-DBDNAME field (line 18) stores the name of the database. PAUT-SEG-LEVEL (line 19) indicates the segment level within the database hierarchy. PAUT-PCB-STATUS (line 20) contains status codes returned by IMS after database operations. PAUT-PCB-PROCOPT (line 21) specifies the processing options used for database access. PAUT-SEG-NAME (line 23) holds the name of the current segment being processed. PAUT-KEYFB-NAME (line 24) and PAUT-NUM-SENSEGS (line 25) store the length of the key feedback area and the number of sensitive segments, respectively. PAUT-KEYFB (line 26) is a large field that contains the key feedback information for the current segment. This copybook is likely included in programs that interact with an IMS database to access and manipulate data within the database segments.
paragraphs.dead_code_reason: Record layout 'PAUTBPCB' is never used by any program
